definitions: A philosophical problem posed by Zeno of Elea, suggesting that motion is impossible because an object in flight is at any given instant at a specific location, and thus at rest.
The paradox that an arrow in flight is motionless at every instant of its journey, and therefore cannot be moving.
examples: We discussed Zeno's arrow paradox in our philosophy class.
The professor used Zeno's arrow paradox to illustrate the complexities of motion and time.
Understanding Zeno's arrow paradox requires grappling with the nature of infinity.
